A network engineer thinks about a channel differently than a sailor does.
Here, a channel is a path for messages, not water, but the same word
survives because the idea of "a narrow way for something to flow" never
really changes.

Every incoming connection gets handed to a worker thread from a pool.
The pool exists so the main thread never blocks waiting on slow clients.

When the last channel handle is dropped, any thread still listening on the
other end wakes up, sees no more senders, and exits its loop cleanly.
